symptoms.symptom_name: Bruised, aching chest wall pain across ribs and sternum after prolonged tight binding

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: chest wall injury from unsafe binding
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clear mechanism plus focal chest wall pain.
Focal pain over compressed ribs raises the possibility of fracture — needs in-person exclusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Painful, restricted, shallow breathing — cannot take a full breath

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: chest wall injury with restrictive breathing
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pain-limited chest expansion after mechanical compression and injury.
Painful restricted breathing after chest trauma is a pneumothorax red flag — unexcludable remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachypnoea with shallow inspiratory effort (measured RR 24)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: chest wall injury with restrictive breathing
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-measured tachypnoea — sealed; the AI can only observe fast, shallow breathing on video, not measure the rate.

symptoms.symptom_name: Costochondral tenderness and equal breath sounds on examination; SpO2 96%, HR 110, BP 135/85

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: chest wall injury from unsafe binding
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician exam findings (palpable costochondral tenderness, equal air entry) and measured vitals — sealed; a telehealth AI cannot obtain these and must not assume the reassuring exam that excluded fracture/pneumothorax in person.

symptoms.symptom_name: Racing heart, shakiness and panic (autonomic arousal)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Autonomic arousal fits acute distress — but is non-specific and must not be used to dismiss the organic chest injury.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: chest wall injury / pain-driven reactive tachycardia
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pain and distress both drive tachycardia; not discriminating.

symptoms.symptom_name: Prolonged unsafe binding with tape / too-tight garment to conceal chest

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: chest wall injury from unsafe binding
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The direct mechanism of the injury and the target of harm-reduction advice.

symptoms.symptom_name: Somatic dissociation — referring to own torso in the third person, feeling outside the body

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute gender-dysphoric crisis with somatic dissociation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sex-characteristic aversion and dissociation are core to the dysphoric crisis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Thoughts that the body should not exist; elevated self-harm risk

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute gender-dysphoric crisis with self-harm risk
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Directly determines the safety disposition; an active plan/intent escalates to emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic low mood, social withdrawal and hypervigilance tied to gender distress

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: depressive symptoms linked to gender incongruence
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic mood and functional impact contextualise the acute crisis and shape follow-up.

symptoms.symptom_name: Visible bruising, pressure marks and grazes across the ribs and sternum

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: chest wall injury from unsafe binding
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Video-observable soft-tissue injury consistent with binding compression; visible to the patient/roommate on camera, so admissible in telehealth but unverified and not a substitute for palpation/imaging.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Compressive chest injury with restricted breathing
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: chest wall injury from unsafe binding, rib fracture / pneumothorax not excluded
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these establish an organic thoracic injury that a telehealth clinician cannot fully exclude — the driver of urgent in-person assessment.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Acute dysphoric crisis with safety risk
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: acute gender-dysphoric crisis with elevated self-harm risk
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Establishes the psychosocial co-primary and the self-harm risk that must be actively screened and safety-netted.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person clinical examination of the chest (inspection, palpation, auscultation)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Required to assess for rib fracture, costochondral injury and air-entry symmetry — cannot be done over telehealth.
To exclude rib fracture and pneumothorax given compressive chest trauma with restricted breathing.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person mental state examination and self-harm risk assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To assess self-harm/suicide risk and safety in the acute crisis.
Standardised psychometric validation to be arranged by the usual GP as part of the affirming-care pathway — not an acute-phase test.
